title: Reverse Bits
description: Given a 32-bit unsigned integer n, return the number n with its bits reversed. For example, if n is 00000010100101000001111010011100, the output should be 00111001011110000010100101000000.

examples.explanation: The binary representation of 43261596 is 00000010100101000001111010011100. Reversing the bits gives 00111001011110000010100101000000, which is 964176192 in decimal.
The binary representation of 4294967293 is 11111111111111111111111111111101. Reversing the bits results in 10111111111111111111111111111111, equal to 3221225471.
The binary representation of 1 is 00000000000000000000000000000001. Reversing the bits results in 10000000000000000000000000000000, which is 2147483648 in decimal.

hints: Consider using bit manipulation techniques to reverse the bits.
You can process the bits one by one and build the result gradually.
Think about how you can use bit shifts to move bits into their new positions.
Consider using a loop or recursion depending on your comfort with either approach.
